current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

caution_notes: Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing is tough due to very warm water temperatures, but recent stocking offers some fishing opportunities. Expect trout to seek cooler, oxygenated areas and feed cautiously near cover.
generated_text.best_window: The day 1 morning window is best because it offers moderate sky cover and low wind gusts, providing more stable conditions for trout to feed along runs and seams.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS reports water temperature at 79.7°F, flow at 97.7 cfs, gage height at 3.69 ft, and turbidity at 8.6 NTU, indicating warm and somewhat turbid water.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current sky cover averages 22%, pressure is steady at 30.05 inHg with no clear trend, winds average 4.5 mph with gusts up to 13.8 mph, and rain probability is low at 15%.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor cold-water refuges, oxygenated zones, and structural seams, feeding cautiously in these areas. Use smaller natural nymphs and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell nymphs with clean drifts. Warm water increases stress, so consider not targeting trout aggressively.
